medications.name: Retatrutide
medications.dosing: STK-11: (starter vial); 4 mg; 0.5 ml; 2 ml; 16 mg; STK-13: (titrate vial); 12 mg; 0.5 ml; 2 ml; 48 mg
medications.description: An investigational next-generation metabolic therapy designed to activate three key hormone pathways involved in appetite control, glucose regulation, energy balance, and fat metabolism: GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon. This triple-action mechanism is what makes retatrutide especially exciting compared with earlier incretin therapies. In clinical research, retatrutide has shown substantial weight-loss potential, with higher-dose groups in phase 2 obesity trials averaging up to 24.2% body-weight reduction over 48 weeks. Its broader metabolic signaling may also support meaningful fat-mass reduction while helping clients pursue a more complete body-composition strategy when paired with adequate protein intake, resistance training, and clinician-guided monitoring.
medications.indication: May fit clients seeking advanced metabolic and weight-management support when appetite, cravings, insulin resistance, or body-composition goals have not responded adequately to lifestyle changes or prior GLP-1/GIP therapies. Retatrutide may be especially relevant for clients focused not only on scale weight, but also on improving fat loss while protecting strength, lean tissue, and long-term metabolic health through a structured plan that includes nutrition, resistance training, and follow-up monitoring.

medications.name: Retatrutide
medications.dosing: STK-11: (starter vial); 4 mg; 0.5 ml; 2 ml; 16 mg; STK-13: (titrate vial); 12 mg; 0.5 ml; 2 ml; 48 mg
medications.description: An investigational next-generation metabolic therapy designed to activate three key hormone pathways involved in appetite control, glucose regulation, energy balance, and fat metabolism: GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon. This triple-action mechanism is what makes retatrutide especially exciting compared with earlier incretin therapies. In clinical research, retatrutide has shown substantial weight-loss potential, with higher-dose groups in phase 2 obesity trials averaging up to 24.2% body-weight reduction over 48 weeks. Its broader metabolic signaling may also support meaningful fat-mass reduction while helping clients pursue a more complete body-composition strategy when paired with adequate protein intake, resistance training, and clinician-guided monitoring.
medications.indication: May fit clients seeking advanced metabolic and weight-management support when appetite, cravings, insulin resistance, or body-composition goals have not responded adequately to lifestyle changes or prior GLP-1/GIP therapies. Retatrutide may be especially relevant for clients focused not only on scale weight, but also on improving fat loss while protecting strength, lean tissue, and long-term metabolic health through a structured plan that includes nutrition, resistance training, and follow-up monitoring.

medications.indication: May fit clients seeking advanced metabolic and weight-management support when appetite, cravings, insulin resistance, or body-composition goals have not responded adequately to lifestyle changes or prior GLP-1/GIP therapies.
